column2: Clicking, grinding, or scraping noises are warning signs associated with {{c1::mechanical hard drive failure}}.
column3: Noise is a clue, not a complete diagnosis. Confirm with data protection, inspection, and diagnostics when appropriate.
column4:
Why

Mechanical drives contain moving parts. This compact recall target supports the scenario cards about recognizing serious drive symptoms.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.50; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.2
column9: A+::220-1201::5.2 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingStorageDevices Cloze HDD Storage Memory Source::Messer-v170

column2: Before risky repair work on a suspected failing drive, the priority is {{c1::getting a current backup if possible}}.
column3: Backups should be verified before actions that may write heavily to unstable storage.
column4:
Why

This is one of the highest-value storage troubleshooting decisions. The goal is not to "fix first"; the goal is to avoid turning a storage symptom into avoidable data loss.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.50; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.2
column9: A+::220-1201::5.2 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingStorageDevices Cloze HighYield Backup Storage Memory Source::Messer-v170

column2: S.M.A.R.T. stands for {{c1::Self-Monitoring, Analysis, and Reporting Technology}}.
column3: Use S.M.A.R.T. as a warning system for drive health trends, not as a guarantee that a drive will or will not fail.
column4:
Why

The acronym is a compact recall fact, but the troubleshooting value is acting on warning trends before users lose access to data.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.51; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.2
column9: A+::220-1201::5.2 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingStorageDevices Cloze SMART Memory Source::Messer-v170
